summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Chatwoot MCP Server iteration 2 - all new features working perfectly. Successfully tested 51 MCP tools (up from 48), new webhook endpoints, advanced conversation filtering, file upload support, and Docker deployment files. Both backend APIs and frontend UI are fully functional with excellent integration.
passed_tests: GET /api/tools returns all 51 MCP tools (up from 48) with correct structure
New tools verification: filter_conversations_advanced, create_message_with_attachment, setup_webhook_listener all present
POST /api/webhooks/receive accepts JSON webhook events and stores them
GET /api/webhooks/events/history returns stored webhook events with test_event found
GET /api/webhooks/events SSE stream endpoint accessible for real-time events
POST /api/tools/execute with filter_conversations_advanced returns filtered conversation data
POST /api/tools/execute-with-file endpoint exists and accepts multipart form data
POST /api/tools/execute with setup_webhook_listener tool creates webhooks successfully
GET /api/mcp/info returns correct tools count (51) and transport details
Frontend loads with 3 navigation tabs: Tools, Filters, Webhooks
Tools tab displays 51 tools with correct count shown in sidebar
Filters tab shows conversation filter builder with attribute/operator/value dropdowns
Filter builder execution works - returns real conversation data in terminal panel
Webhooks tab shows webhook events panel with LIVE badge and real-time events
Navigation between all 3 tabs works smoothly with correct active state styling
Webhook events display test_event entries from backend testing
Dockerfile exists with valid structure (FROM, WORKDIR, EXPOSE, CMD, HEALTHCHECK)
docker-compose.yml exists with MongoDB and chatwoot-mcp services properly configured
All required backend files present for Docker build (requirements.txt, server.py)
